bestRetention.title: Why the Wood Frog Freezes Solid and Survives #Shorts
Why the Owl Hunts in Total Silence #Shorts

titleLessons.lesson: Curiosity-gap question titles ("Why does this [animal] have [weird trait]?") are a better bet than plain descriptors, but this specific test was inconclusive due to zero traffic; variant was kept.
titleLessons.winner: Why Does This Fish Have White Blood? #Shorts

summary.pattern: "Why the [animal] [surprising ability]" is the right frame to keep using
Underwater/less-familiar animals may need better immediate visual proof

ideas.title: Why the Arctic Ground Squirrel Doesn’t Freeze to Death #Shorts
ideas.concept: Explain the ground squirrel’s extreme cold tolerance and how it survives sub-zero body temps during hibernation.
ideas.hook: "This animal lets its body temperature drop below freezing… and lives."
ideas.keyVisual: Close-up ground squirrel in snowy tundra (breathing visible), quick cut to burrow/hibernation B-roll; readable ice/snow context for instant proof.

ideas.title: Why the Peregrine Falcon Hits 200+ mph #Shorts
ideas.concept: Break down the ‘superpower’ of the fastest animal: the falcon’s dive (stoop) mechanics and airflow adaptations.
ideas.hook: "Fastest animal on Earth—here’s the trick that makes it possible."
ideas.keyVisual: High-speed peregrine falcon dive footage (silhouette against sky), cut to talons/impact on prey dummy or prey capture shot; strong motion in first second.

ideas.title: Why the Owl’s Face Works Like a Satellite Dish #Shorts
ideas.concept: A follow-up to the winning owl theme: how the facial disc funnels sound and helps pinpoint prey in darkness.
ideas.hook: "An owl’s face isn’t for looks—it’s a sound-catching tool."
ideas.keyVisual: Front-on owl head close-up (clear facial disc), quick cut to owl turning head toward faint sound; use night-forest ambience style footage.
